title: Catalytic efficiency vs substrate heavy-atom count (complexity)
interpretation: Using a framework-INDEPENDENT complexity proxy (substrate heavy-atom count), log(kcat/Km) anti-correlates with substrate complexity: Pearson r=-0.67 (p=0.049), Spearman r=-0.40. The diffusion-limited enzymes (kcat/Km>=1e8) act on simple substrates (mean 11.8 heavy atoms) while slow enzymes act on complex substrates (mean 12.2). Sign and rough magnitude match the framework's claimed r~-0.57. CAVEAT: this is a real but WEAK trend (r^2=0.45), substrate heavy-atom count is only one proxy for 'categorical distance', and the enzyme set is small/curated. The defensible claim is a qualitative anti-correlation, NOT a precise law.
